what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard design note that states an ergonomic constraint for a mobile UI feature: the bottom thumb-arc is the only viable real estate for frequent actions, while top-left placement conflicts with one-handed use.
observations: The note argues that top-left is the worst one-handed thumb target on a phone.
It introduces the principle that frequency determines altitude: frequent actions belong in the bottom/third thumb zone, rare/deliberate actions can live at the top.
Document switching is treated as rare, so a top-left hamburger/document switcher is tolerable there.
The V control must sit in the bottom thumb-arc and be side-aware or configurable.
The V should open toward the thumb and yield/dim while a long-press gesture is armed.
The level-scrubber chip should render opposite the contact point to avoid thumb collision.
It is linked as part_of board://item-a5b7cf79.
standouts: The call for the V position to be side-aware/configurable, not just bloom-direction aware.
The explicit collision rule: V yields while a gesture is armed.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Uses markdown section headings and bullet lists with bold emphasis.
References another board item via a part_of link.
Written as a design critique with numbered section heading.
concerns: The title appears truncated ('the V and the gesture compete for the same real e').
Several key terms ('MSG2', 'Tim', 'BD-G', 'V', 'level-scrubber') are not defined within the file.
The note is opinionated ergonomic advice without cited data or acceptance criteria.
It implicitly assumes a right-handed grip before mentioning side-awareness.
notes: A mobile-ergonomics design constraint arguing that frequent actions must stay in the bottom thumb zone and that the V control should be side-aware and yield during gestures.
questions: Who is Tim and what is MSG2?
What is the level-scrubber and how is it triggered?
Which platform and form factors does this advice target?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is titled as a thumb-zone reality note and it delivers exactly that — ergonomic constraints tying hamburger, V, and level-scrubber placement together.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to prevent a top-left hamburger/document switcher from undermining one-handed use by anchoring frequent actions in the bottom thumb-arc and making the V control configurable and gesture-aware.
novelty: It explicitly treats V position as side-aware/configurable and pairs it with a dynamic level-chip placement rule and a V-yield-during-gesture rule.
contribution: A concrete ergonomic principle and specific placement rules for the V, document switcher, and level-chip that a fused system would keep as interaction constraints.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design note item-d5691851 articulating mobile thumb-zone constraints for a V gesture, hamburger/document switcher, and level-scrubber, arguing that frequent actions belong in the bottom thumb-arc and the V should be side-aware and yield during armed gestures.
intention_for_embedding: This item was filed to record an ergonomic reality check for a mobile UI feature, pushing frequent controls into the thumb zone and making the V control configurable and gesture-collision aware.
